title: crassula natalensis
intro: Crassula natalensis, also known as the "Buchu" plant, is a small, evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of South Africa. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating urinary tract issues and as a diuretic to help relieve kidney stones and bladder problems. The health benefits of Crassula natalensis include reducing inflammation and fighting bacterial infections, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Traditionally, the leaves of the plant are prepared as an herbal tea, often combined with other plants, and also used in the form of tinctures and capsules.
